results.possibleIdentity: Joseph H. Melhado, Jr. (c. 1906–?) was an engineer and technical professional active from the late 1920s through the 1970s. He graduated from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) as a member of the Class of 1928 and later served as the Secretary for that class, contributing regularly to the MIT Technology Review. Professionally, he was a Project Engineer at EMTECH Research Products Corporation, located at 125 Beechwood Avenue in New Rochelle, New York. His technical expertise included digital instrumentation, optics, and photo-data processing, fields in which EMTECH was active (notably through its acquisition of the Voland Company and its work with the Mathatron computer/calculator interfaces). Melhado was also a licensed amateur radio operator with the call sign WA2GTP, residing for many years at 24 Rodney Road in Scarsdale and later in Hartsdale, New York. In the mid-1970s, he appeared to transition into a role involving municipal finance or budget management for the City of New Rochelle. He is historically associated with technical collaborations during the late 1960s and early 1970s, potentially including service to the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which facilitated partnerships between engineers and artists.
